name: flutter-checkpoint
description: Saves progress snapshots to track development progress and enable safe refactoring with checkpoint comparison

purpose.overview: Captures a comprehensive snapshot of your project's current state, including test execution status, coverage metrics, build status, and code changes. Helps track progress over time, safely perform risky refactoring by creating restore points, and compare current state against previous checkpoints to identify improvements or regressions.
useCases: At the end of each development session
Before and after major changes to compare impact
To track progress toward quality goals

description.description: Brief description of this checkpoint (e.g., 'Completed user authentication', 'Before refactoring payment service')

compare.description: Compare current state with a previous checkpoint (use timestamp from --list)

list.description: List all saved checkpoints in reverse chronological order

show.description: Display details of a specific checkpoint

projectPath.description: Absolute path to the Flutter project to analyze. If not provided, uses the current workspace directory.

bestPractices.defaultProjectLocalStorage: By default, checkpoints save to Claude's project-local directory: .claude/flutter-dev-assistant/checkpoints/
recommendedWorkflow: Commit to version control: git add .claude/ && git commit -m 'checkpoint: After optimization'
keyAdvantages: All Claude project data stays in the workspace
Can be committed to version control and shared with team
No dependency on user global config
Safe refactoring with easy rollback reference
idealUseCases: Before risky refactoring: Create checkpoint before major changes
End of day: Snapshot progress at end of development session
After features: Mark completion of significant features
Before releases: Capture stable baseline before release
Team collaboration: Commit checkpoints to track team progress

recommendations: Keep checkpoints for major milestones (feature completions)
Keep monthly snapshots for long-term tracking
Manually delete old checkpoints from directory as needed

improvementDetection: Build status changed from failed to success
regressionDetection: Build status changed from success to failed

fileNamingConvention.note: ISO 8601 timestamp format with colons replaced by hyphens

notes: Checkpoints are lightweight JSON files (typically < 5KB each)
Creating a checkpoint takes 20-60 seconds depending on project size
Checkpoints can be committed to git for team-wide progress tracking
Use descriptive checkpoint descriptions to make them easy to identify later
Consider creating checkpoints at regular intervals (daily, weekly) for long-term trend analysis
Checkpoint comparison is most useful when comparing adjacent checkpoints (before/after a change)
Next steps are automatically generated based on current state
For CI/CD integration, checkpoints can be created automatically after each successful build
Checkpoint data can be used to generate progress reports and quality trend charts

relatedAssistants.description: Helps improve test coverage between checkpoints
Helps fix build errors detected in checkpoints
Provides guidance on architectural improvements based on checkpoint trends

relatedCommands.description: Run comprehensive verification before creating a checkpoint
Create an implementation plan with checkpoints at each phase
Coordinate multiple assistants with checkpoints between phases

requirements: Requirement 3.1: Capture current test execution status
Requirement 3.4: Generate a summary of recent code changes
Requirement 3.5: Identify and record next steps
Requirement 3.6: Store checkpoint with timestamp and description
Requirement 3.7: Show comparison between current state and previous checkpoints

benefits: All project data stays within the workspace
Can be committed to version control
Easy to share with team members
No dependency on user global config
Keeps Claude config separate from Kiro

global.description: Use --platform=global flag (not recommended - data is outside project workspace)

steps.name: Test Execution
steps.description: Runs fvm flutter test to capture current test status (total, passed, failed, skipped)

steps.name: Coverage Capture
steps.description: Runs fvm flutter test --coverage and parses coverage data (overall, critical paths, business logic)

steps.name: Build Status
steps.description: Executes fvm flutter build debug to verify current build status

steps.name: Code Changes
steps.description: Analyzes recent git commits to generate summary of code changes (files modified, lines added/removed)

steps.name: Next Steps
steps.description: Identifies logical next steps based on current state

steps.name: Checkpoint Storage
steps.description: Saves all captured data to project-local directory

steps.name: Comparison
steps.description: If --compare flag used: Calculates deltas between current state and specified checkpoint, highlighting improvements and regressions
